response.justification: James is a versatile, experienced engineer with genuine developer-tooling interest — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners and language libraries. However, his core stack is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, with only minor Java exposure, no Kotlin, no Spring or enterprise JVM framework experience, and no deep JVM background. The role's required qualifications center on exactly those gaps, making him a poor fit despite his strong general engineering craft.
strengths: Prior IDE development experience (C/C++ components for NI LabWindows/CVI)
Strong software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, testing tools, open-source tooling)
Broad polyglot experience across web, desktop, embedded, and high-performance systems
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only marginal Java exposure; lacks deep JVM expertise
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise Java framework background
Recent work (TypeScript/Node.js product engineering) is far from JVM tooling domain

key_factors.factor: IDE internals and language tooling experience
